content.claim: Finland's historical equity gains from 1970s de-tracking reforms have partially reversed since approximately 2000, coinciding with rising school choice and selective classes, though causal attribution is contested across multiple co-occurring systemic changes.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Correlation documented but causality not isolated; period includes immigration, urbanization, and curriculum changes
School-choice mechanism is theoretically linked to segregation but requires separate controlled analysis to establish causal magnitude
No direct evidence that school choice alone (rather than residential segregation or other factors) drives achievement-gap widening
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
